purpose: Defines the !review slash command — code review of recent changes (uncommitted, staged, or specific commits) WITH full project context. Identifies changed files via git diff (no arg = all uncommitted, 'staged' = git diff --staged, range like HEAD~3..HEAD = git diff range, directory = scoped diff). Reads each changed file fully (not just diff) plus project CLAUDE.md, finds consumers of changed shared types/endpoints. Reviews for code quality (CLAUDE.md convention compliance, bugs, edge cases, error handling, security), cross-layer impact (broken contracts, shared types, missing co-changes, env/config), and completeness (related files needing updates, missing tests, doc updates). Outputs Issues (Critical/Warning/Suggestion) + Cross-Layer Impact + Missing Changes + Summary. Read-only.
gotchas: Use git diff against base/range — do NOT scan full repo. The argument controls the diff range.
Read the FULL file (not just the diff) for context — diff hunks alone miss surrounding logic.
Cross-layer impact is the unique value: single-file bugs are easy; contract breakage across frontend/backend/config is what this command catches.
Be honest, not harsh — flag REAL issues, don't nitpick style preferences not in the project's CLAUDE.md.
Conventions sourced from project CLAUDE.md — if absent, fall back to obvious code-quality issues.
patterns: Diff-scoped reading: identify changed files → read each fully → check consumers of touched types/endpoints.
Three-axis review: code quality / cross-layer impact / completeness.
conventions: Argument forms: blank, 'staged', commit range, directory.
